rulings.Case Title: KENT ALLEN ET AL VS. LISA NAGEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Of Lisa Nagel?S And Amy Thorne?S (1) Petition To Confirm A Contractual Arbitration Award And Request For Attorneys? Fees And Costs, And (2) Motion To Set Aside The Court?S February 9, 2021 Fees And Costs Award In Favor Of Allen And Lavelle
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2023 line 1. NOTICE OF HEARING OF LISA NAGEL'S AND AMY THORNE'S (1) PETITION TO CONFIRM A CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD AND REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS, AND (2) MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE COURT'S FEBRUARY 9, 2021 FEES AND COSTS AWARD IN FAVOR OF ALLEN AND LAVELLE is moot. The award has been paid in full. Moving party fails to explain its entitlement to daily interest at the rate of $27.59 per day from September 21, 2023 to October 4, 2023 having admitted that the principal had been paid off on September 21, 2023. Request for fees and costs incurred for bringing this motion is granted in the amount requested. The payment of the principal and interest was made after the filing of the motion. February 9, 2021 order is set aside and the moving party is entitle to refund of any payments made there on. The judgement roll indicates that there was no valid proof of service on file at the time of the February 9, 2021 hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Fourth Amended Complaint shall be filed and served forthwith. Per express stipulation of the parties the pending Motion for Summary Adjudication will apply to the Fourth Amended Complaint and will remain on December 7, 2023 calendar. Trial is continued to April 18, 2024. rulings.Case Title: ANGELO ARCILLA VS. LATANYA DRISDALE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 25, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT LATANYA DRISDALE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is moot. Demurrer properly raised a potential issue of inconsistency between allegations in the Complaint and Supplemental Complaint/Cover Sheet. It appears that Plaintiff was alerted the inconsistency via the demurrer and corrected the omission by re-filing the form properly filled out. Re-filing of form UD-101 resolved the perceived inconsistency. Moving party cites no authority for the proposition that UD-101 is not subject to CCP 472 authorizing one amendment without leave of court. The operative pleadings are now comprised of the August 9, 2022 Complaint and October 9, 2023 UD-101. Defendant shall have 5 days to respond to the operative pleading. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
